name: Content Warnings
description: Automatically tags movies and TV shows with content warnings using AI. Supports .nfo migration, SQLite storage, and real-time tagging.
overview: Scans your Jellyfin library and adds content warning tags (violence, language, etc.) to help users make informed viewing choices. Uses Groq AI for intelligent content analysis.

versions.changelog: Major update: SQLite database storage, .nfo file migration, improved library status API with caching, write-back to .nfo files for Kodi/Infuse compatibility, async/await throughout.
Fixed ApiClient.ajax JSON parsing issues by switching to native fetch with X-Emby-Authorization header. Improved config page reliability.
Complete rewrite of config page with native fetch, library status table with search/filters, per-item rescan, grouped series/episodes display.
Fixed duplicate DLL issue from mixing catalog install + manual copy. Improved deployment process.
Added library status endpoint, reprocess single item API, improved config page with status display.
Fixed GitHub Action to use publish_output folder, preventing stale DLL issues. Improved build reliability.
Added configuration page with API key input, library settings, and quick actions. Improved scheduled task.
Initial release with Groq AI integration, automatic content warning tagging for movies and TV shows.
